name: David J. Broadhurst
affiliation_listed_in_cover_letter: The Open University, Milton Keynes, United Kingdom

filter_2_1_identity_confirmation.actual_affiliation_per_fetch: The Open University, UK (per Rutgers Experimental Mathematics Seminar 2025-02-06 listing and Open Research Online repository djb2.html / djb3.html).
filter_2_1_identity_confirmation.fetch_status: Open University staff page returned HTTP 403 on direct fetch; identity and affiliation confirmed via secondary sources (Rutgers seminar listing for 6 Feb 2025; Springer proceedings 2025 chapter; carmamaths.org preprint hosting).

filter_A_exp_math_editorial_overlap.rationale: Direct EM board fetch returned 403. Targeted web_search for Broadhurst on EM board returned 'no evidence' of board membership. He has published IN the journal (e.g., the Borwein-Straub-Wan-Zudilin paper appeared in EM 22(1) 2013 — though Broadhurst is not on that paper). The journal's current EIC is Al Kasprzyk.

filter_B_bbc_cluster.verdict: DEFINITIVE FAIL by direct coauthorship with cluster head Bailey
filter_B_bbc_cluster.rationale: Bailey-Broadhurst 2001 — 'Parallel integer relation detection: techniques and applications', Mathematics of Computation 70, no. 236, 1719--1736 — is a direct two-author paper between Broadhurst and cluster head David H. Bailey. This paper is cited as the manuscript's reference [3]. Per the task's Filter B rules, direct coauthorship with any of {Bailey, Borwein, Crandall, Ferguson, Plouffe} is an automatic FAIL.
yellow_flags: Broadhurst's recent work (e.g., 'Reciprocal PSLQ and the tiny Nome of Bologna', preprint on carmamaths.org) is hosted on Borwein-legacy infrastructure, suggesting continued community embedding.

filter_C_recent_activity.rationale: Confirmed active 2024-2025: book chapter 'Multivariate Elliptic Kites and Tetrahedral Tadpoles' in Lie Theory and Its Applications in Physics, Springer Proceedings vol 473 (2025), pp 43-58; invited talk 'Resurgent integer sequences' at Rutgers Experimental Mathematics Seminar, 6 Feb 2025; 'Richard Feynman's talent for finding things out', J. Phys. Conf. Ser. 2877 (2024) 012024.
relevant_publications_recent_24mo: Multivariate Elliptic Kites and Tetrahedral Tadpoles (Springer 2025)
Richard Feynman's talent for finding things out (J. Phys. Conf. Ser. 2024)
Resurgent integer sequences (Rutgers EM Seminar talk 2025)

filter_D_geographic.primary_affiliation: The Open University, Milton Keynes, UK
filter_D_geographic.set_level_diversity_contribution: UK — non-AU; satisfies set-level non-AU requirement on its own.

filter_E_prior_interaction_with_author.rationale: No public coauthorship or acknowledgment trail. Papanokechi's only public artefacts are Zenodo deposits with no Broadhurst mention.

filter_F_baustms_fit.rationale: Established mathematical physicist; long publication record in experimental mathematics and PSLQ; no retraction flags. Natural BAustMS reviewer if Filter B did not block.

blocking_filter: Filter B (direct coauthorship with cluster head Bailey, ref [3] of manuscript)
summary: Broadhurst is an excellent topical fit for a PSLQ paper -- he literally co-developed parallel PSLQ with Bailey, and that co-development paper is cited in our manuscript. That same fact disqualifies him under Filter B (direct cluster coauthorship). Filters A, C, D, E, F all pass; Filter B is dispositive.
